text: GCC EMERGENCY SUMMIT: Gulf leaders meet in Jeddah for first in-person gathering since war began. Joint statement demands 'urgent and unimpeded' Hormuz reopening. UAE reportedly pushed hardest — now confirmed quitting OPEC. Qatar's Al Thani raised LNG concerns. Saudi MBS quiet but signaling frustration with both Iran and US.

KALLAS: EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials/entities + widens sanctions to Hormuz blockers. Von der Leyen: 'too early for sanctions relief.' EU has spent an extra 25 billion euros on energy since the war started. EU naval mission bolstered.

TREASURY: US sanctions 35 entities in Iran's shadow banking architecture. Also issues guidance warning about sanctions exposure for making 'toll' payments to Iran/IRGC for Hormuz passage. This closes a loophole some shipping firms were exploiting.
verificationNote: US Treasury press release, Townhall. Confirmed.

text: IDF HEZBOLLAH UPDATE: IDF confirms ~20 strikes on Beqaa Valley/Qantara targets — biggest since April 16 ceasefire. Two 2km cross-border tunnels destroyed. Built with 'direct guidance from Iran' over a decade. This is the 3rd major ceasefire violation wave since April 16.

GUTERRES at UNSC: 'Open the Strait. Let ships pass. No tolls. No discrimination. Let trade resume. Let the global economy breathe.' Called Hormuz closure worst supply chain disruption since COVID-19. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. Pope Leo separately decries 'delusion of omnipotence.'

MARKETS PM CORRECTION: WTI corrected to $99.62 (+31.93% vs pre-war $75.51) per CNBC/Trading Economics — 7th straight up day, intraday high $101.81. Brent stable $111.16 (+39.23%). AAA national gas $4.176 (+33.4% vs $3.13 — earlier $4.031 estimate revised on AAA's actual reading). IEA Birol: ~15.8M bpd of crude stranded. Goldman Q4 Brent forecast at $90.
verificationNote: CNBC, Trading Economics, AAA, IEA via Fortune. Confirmed.

text: DAY 60 AFTERNOON PT ROUNDUP: Trump Truth Social: Iran 'state of collapse,' attacks Merz over nuclear weapons. Rubio: Hormuz an 'economic nuclear weapon.' IDF strikes ~20 Beqaa/Qantara Hezbollah targets — destroys 2 cross-border tunnels. Pope Leo: 'delusion of omnipotence.' Pezeshkian-Sharif call. Brent $111.16. WTI $99.62 (corrected). AAA gas $4.176. Hormuz Day 57. War Powers May 1 — 3 days.
verificationNote: Fox News, RT, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, JPost, CNBC, AAA. All verified.

text: BREAKING: UAE announces exit from OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1. Strips cartel of 3rd-largest producer after ~60 years. Energy Minister cites 'national interests' and frustration with production caps at ~3.2M bpd. Decision NOT coordinated with Saudi Arabia. Biggest structural change to oil markets in decades.
verificationNote: CNN, WaPo, NPR, Al Jazeera. Confirmed.

text: HEZBOLLAH DRONE KILLS CONTRACTOR: Israeli Defense Ministry contractor Amer Hujairat (44, Shfaram) killed by Hezbollah explosive drone in Aitaroun, S. Lebanon. Son (19) lightly wounded. Hezbollah claims targeting bulldozer demolishing homes. Separately, 2 IDF soldiers wounded (1 seriously) by drone on April 28 — announced today. Ceasefire increasingly fragile.
verificationNote: Haaretz, JPost, Times of Israel. Confirmed.

text: HOUTHI RECORD RANGE: Houthis attack MSC Orion in Arabian Sea approximately 600km off Yemen coast — furthest Houthi attack on a vessel ever. EU Operation Atalanta advises rerouting ships 150nm east. Separately, MV Cyclades hit in Red Sea during 7-hour assault with 3 ASBMs + Samad-1 loitering munition. Houthis' reach expanding dangerously.
verificationNote: Maritime Executive, Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List. Confirmed.

text: OIL MARKETS: WTI tops $100 ($100.16, +32.6%) for first time since April 10 — 8th straight up day. Brent $111.57 (+39.7%). World Bank warns of 24% energy price surge in 2026 — largest since 2022; Brent could hit $115 if infrastructure sustains more damage. S&P 500 at record levels despite war — AI/tech driving resilience. AAA gas $4.18 (+33.5%).
verificationNote: Trading Economics, CNBC, Euronews (World Bank), AAA. Confirmed.

text: WAR POWERS DEADLINE: May 1 is 2 days away — 60-day War Powers Resolution clock expiring. Democrats exploring suing Trump if he continues without congressional authorization. Five previous resolutions defeated. Senate 46-51. Key swing votes: Collins, others. Iran Army spokesman: 'Conditions remain wartime.' Haredi rioters storm Military Police chief's home — 25 arrested.
verificationNote: Military.com, Time, Al Jazeera (War Powers). Al Jazeera liveblog (Iran army). Times of Israel, Haaretz (Haredi). Confirmed.

text: DAY 61 PM PT ROUNDUP: UAE quits OPEC effective May 1. Hezbollah drone kills contractor in Aitaroun (son wounded); 2 IDF soldiers wounded by drone. Houthis hit MV Cyclades + MSC Orion at record 600km. US Treasury sanctions 35 entities. EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials. Guterres: 'Open the Strait.' Iran bans steel exports. Brent $111.57. WTI $100.16. Gas $4.18. Hormuz Day 58. War Powers May 1 — 2 days.
verificationNote: CNN, Haaretz, Maritime Executive, US Treasury, Kallas/X, UN News, Trading Economics, AAA. All verified.
